description_long: This triumphal arch is constructed from a material resembling weathered stone, with a robust and blocky design. The archway is supported by two thick, rectangular piers, each composed of stacked, slightly protruding segments that give a sense of mass and solidity. The top of the arch features a peaked, angular keystone area, rather than a smooth curve, lending a stylized and monumental appearance. The surface is matte and slightly rough, with subtle color variations in shades of gray and brown, mimicking natural stone. There are no visible decorative reliefs, sculptures, or inscription panels, emphasizing a utilitarian and austere aesthetic. The structure is open in the center, allowing passage through the archway.
description: A blocky, stone-like triumphal arch with thick piers and a peaked top, finished in matte gray-brown tones and lacking decorative elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full archway, thick segmented piers, and the peaked keystone area.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the narrow depth of the arch and the stacked block construction.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the same archway and blocky piers.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and segmented structure.
